figures.caption: Figure 1: ReAct-based agent constructs environmental understandings from a narrow, linear view. Our DPEPO allows agent to build comprehensive cognition of environments from diverse parallel exploration.

Table 1: Performance comparison on ALFWorld and ScienceWorld. For ALFWorld, we report success rate (%) on seen (in-domain) and unseen (out-of-domain) tasks. For ScienceWorld, we report results on three splits: L0 (seen variants and categories), L1 (unseen variants, seen categories), and L2 (unseen variants and categories). DPEPO achieves superior in-domain performance and strong generalization ability. Bold is best, underlined is second-best.
Figure 19: A Case for Validating Hypotheses via Parallel Exploration.

Figure 4: Average success rate and token budgets on ALFWorld’s seen and unseen splits, under two scaling strategies: (1) increasing sample count for GiGPO, and (2) increasing the number of environments for DPEPO.
Figure 5: Analysis of exploration dynamics across ablation variants on the ALFWorld seen-task split.
Figure 20: A Case for Keep Exploring Even After Finding a Solution.
Figure 2: Training framework of our proposed Diverse Parallel Exploration Policy Optimization (DPEPO).
Figure 15: Last step information used in the “Prompt for Intermediate Step” demonstration.
Figure 14: Historical information used in the “Prompt for Intermediate Step” demonstration.
Figure 21: A Case for Structured Search Across Location Types.

Table 4: Detailed per-task success rate of DPEPO variants on ALFWorld In Domain split, alongside overall results on ScienceWorld. This table complements the summary success rate in Table 2 by providing fine-grained performance across individual ALFWorld tasks.
